_readme: Ground-truth labels for screenshots in data/drop/processed/, hand-verified against the actual image pixels. 'expected' mirrors the exact JSON schema ingestion/health.py's PROMPT asks the vision model to produce, so model output can be diffed field-by-field against it. 'year_visible_in_image' flags whether the source screenshot actually prints a year anywhere (small text under the date) vs. shows none at all -- these are two different failure modes and should probably be scored/reported separately.
_year_inference_rule: When a card shows day/month but no year (year_visible_in_image: false), the year is not recoverable from the image itself -- an ingestion pipeline can only correctly infer it by assuming the current year at ingestion time, rolling back one year if that would put the date in the future (matches ingestion/health.py's _fix_hallucinated_year). For scoring: don't fail a model/pipeline on year for these images if it resolves to what this rule would produce; do fail it if it invents an unrelated year (e.g. 2023) the way the raw model has been observed to do.

images.notes: Small '2026' printed directly under 'Apr 30' on the card. 'Chandler' in the on-screen title is a route/nickname, not the activity type -- activity_type should be 'walking'. No max_hr shown for this card.
Small '2026' printed under 'May 11' on all three cards. Card 2 (Cardio) has no distance/pace. Card 3 (Treadmill Running) shows no max_hr or calories column at all.
Small '2026' printed under 'May 12' on both cards. Card 1 (Strength) has no distance/pace. Card 2 (Treadmill Running) shows no max_hr or calories column.

Detail view only shows 'Jul 6 @ 06:14' -- no year printed anywhere on screen. The '2026-07-06' date below is ground truth confirmed externally (user-verified), not something derivable from the image alone -- flag this image if scoring year accuracy, since a correct model can't actually get the year right here without an out-of-band signal (e.g. assume current year unless that's in the future).
